summary: All 5 implementation steps complete. Sparsemax operator validated (correct forward/backward, exact zeros confirmed). BertHateSpeechClassifier supports conditions C1-C5 via ClassifierConfig. Two bugs found and fixed during preflight: (1) SparsemaxBertSelfAttention.forward signature updated for Transformers 5.x API (past_key_values, **kwargs); (2) KLAlignmentLoss replaced manual computation with F.kl_div for numerical stability. ERASER metrics, adversarial swap, fairness FPR all implemented. Pre-flight: 10/10 PASS.
critical_context: Transformers 5.x BertSelfAttention.forward signature: (hidden_states, attention_mask, past_key_values, **kwargs) -> (attn_output, attn_weights)
SparsemaxBertSelfAttention correctly matches this signature; stores .query, .key, .value, .dropout, .scaling from original
Sparsemax zero count = 0/32 in preflight (with random weights, support typically covers most tokens; zeros appear after training with alignment loss)
MSEAlignmentLoss uses F.mse_loss — works with exact zeros in target
JointLoss: L_total = CE + alpha * L_align (alpha in config)
ERASER masks top-k% by attention weight; comprehensiveness = drop in pred prob when deleted
H4 adversarial swap: replace real tokens with [MASK] (id=103) as uniform attention proxy
CLS attention extracted from final BERT layer (index -1), averaged over supervised_heads
